extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property is violated. The rule interaction_drop has priority 100, far above raise_rash (2) and raise_stiff_neck (3), so whenever vision_loss is true it overrides the other matching rules and sets acuity := self_care, the lowest acuity. Take rash=true, stiff_neck=false. With vision_loss=false, only raise_rash matches, so acuity = urgent. With vision_loss=true (other inputs unchanged), both raise_rash (p2) and interaction_drop (p100) match; the highest-priority rule wins, so acuity = self_care. Setting vision_loss to true lowers acuity from urgent to self_care, which is strictly lower in the listed order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. The witness gives the post-toggle assignment (vision_loss=true, rash=true, stiff_neck=false) yielding self_care versus urgent when vision_loss is false. (An even more dramatic case is stiff_neck=true: emergent drops to self_care.)
